title: How to modify Verilog defines?
body:
Hi. I tried several ways to change Verilog defines (based on the Yosys doc and a few examples that I found through the internet), without success. Could this feature be broken? I am doing something wrong?

## Steps to reproduce the issue

[verilog_defines_issue.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/6644825/verilog_defines_issue.zip)

I provided a simple description and a bash script with all the things that I tried (using an updated Docker container).
Of course, I dealt with this problem in another description, where I saw no changes in the synthesized result.

## Expected behavior

```
-- Running command... --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: defines.v
Parsing Verilog input from `defines.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\Defines'.
aaa = 2
bbb = 3
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.
```

## Actual behavior

```
-- Running command... --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: defines.v
Parsing Verilog input from `defines.v' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\Defines'.
aaa = 1
bbb = 2
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.
```

Which are the default values, not my modifications.

Regards,
Rodrigo
